title: Type query-engine conditions, filter fields, and state predicate
description:
Type the query-engine conditions, filter fields, and state predicate (report stringly findings). Fixes the `state:notastate` fail-open bug.

## Sites
- `query_engine/parser.rs:24-29` ā€” `QueryCondition::State`/`Priority` are raw `String`; the evaluator's `from_str().unwrap_or(false)` fails open (an invalid `state:notastate` silently matches nothing). Change the variants to `State(domain::State)`/`Priority(domain::Priority)` and parse in `parse_condition` with `map_err`. Leave `Label`/`Assignee` as `String` (open values).
- `query_engine/parser.rs:129-136` + `lexer.rs` ā€” `match field.as_str()` over state/label/priority/assignee literals validated nowhere structurally. Add a `FilterField` enum + `FromStr` parsed in the lexer; the parser match becomes exhaustive.
- `validation/rules.rs:439-494` (`StatePredicate`) ā€” stores `Vec<String>` for the closed `State` set; `matches()` round-trips `Stateā†’tokenā†’.to_lowercase()`. Parse tokens to `BTreeSet<State>` at `validate()` time (via the predicate's own whitelist, not `State::from_str` which accepts extra aliases); `matches()` becomes a `contains` check.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] An invalid `state:`/`priority:` filter value is a parse error (clear message), not a silent no-match; covered by a regression test.
- [ ] `QueryCondition::State`/`Priority` hold typed `domain::State`/`Priority`; `Label`/`Assignee` remain `String`.
- [ ] Filter field names are parsed into a `FilterField` enum in the lexer; the parser match over fields is exhaustive (no `_ =>` string fallback).
- [ ] `StatePredicate` stores a typed `BTreeSet<State>` parsed once at validate time; `matches()` does no per-call string round-trip.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass.
